page: Microsoft Azure E32ns_v6 is an Env6 family network-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in memory bandwidth for large blocks, reaching 192.86 GB/sec, and high static web serving throughput of 56.03 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), support specialized workloads. This is reflected in top-tier text generation speeds of 381.68 tokens/sec for small language models and strong prompt processing speeds of 200.75 tokens/sec for medium models. However, large model text generation performance is a relative bottleneck, registering at a weaker 2.56 tokens/sec. General CPU performance, such as the stress-ng multi-core score of 31368.89 and PassMark CPU score of 44596.47, falls within the average tier.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and dedicated compute resources, the E32ns_v6 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and network-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external premium storage attachment. This server is suited for memory-optimized databases, enterprise web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and moderate-scale machine learning inference utilizing CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ns_v6 is a network-optimized, memory-heavy server instance from the Env6 family, running on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores), a 4.2 GHz clock speed, and a 260 MB L3 cache, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. Benchmarks show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth (192.86 GB/sec), static web serving (56.03 GB/sec), and small-model LLM text generation (381.68 tokens/sec), though large-model text generation remains a bottleneck. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise web serving, and CPU-accelerated machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ns_v6 is a network-optimized Env6 server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ns_v6 is a network-optimized server with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, built for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized, memory-heavy virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
Network-optimized architecture with premium storage capability and no local disks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise web applications, and CPU-based inference
